mechanism_narrative: High retail sales reflect strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings. When RSAFS is elevated, it signals robust aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: any regime but strongest in expansion
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between RSAFS level and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or the decile spread of returns sorted by RSAFS is not positive over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% indicates severe labor market slack, reducing household income and consumer confidence. This contractionary signal depresses corporate earnings expectations and results in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment is historically associated with recessions and poor equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8%, forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over out-of-sample data from 1970 onward.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is extremely low and housing starts are above the 500K threshold, the combination signals a stimulative monetary environment with a recovering housing sector. This joint condition boosts economic sentiment and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term in a regression model is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery regime, industrial production growth accelerates as manufacturing activity rebounds, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns. In this regime, rising INDPRO is a reliable bullish signal.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production leads earnings revisions in recovery periods.
failure_condition: In the recovery regime, the Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and moderate level of consumer price inflation, with CPI near 226, indicates no imminent overheating or deflationary risk. This benign inflation environment supports current valuation multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between CPI level and 5-day forward returns is negative, or the mean forward return in weeks with CPI below 230 is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP above the 13,000 threshold signals that the economy is operating at a substantial size, reducing tail-risk of recession. This expansionary output level supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 > 13000, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is ≤ 55% over a 20-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high payroll employment and retail sales indicate a virtuous cycle of job creation and consumer spending. When both series exceed their respective thresholds, the economy is in a self-reinforcing expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both thresholds are met, the frequency of positive 20-day forward returns is below 60%, or the joint signal's precision is less than 55% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound regime where FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, the central bank's accommodative stance provides a powerful support for equity valuations. The near-zero risk-free rate reduces discount rates on future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Zero interest rates have historically lifted equity prices through the Fed put.
failure_condition: During the zero lower bound regime, the mean 20-day forward SPX return is not positive at the 5% significance level, or the regime indicator alone fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.
